The greatest ways that you can protect yourself in business is from the no refund rule. Of course, it comes from ethics in making sure that you are delivering for people and that you are placing proper value in front of people before they tell you that they want to refund. Now, that being said, you have a firm policy that you just established literally right now if you don't already have it of no refunds. If the customer ever tells you that they want to refund, you look them in the eyes or you talk to them on the phone very firm and almost upset, they would have the audacity to ask you for such a request. When you know and you're convicted on the fact that you have delivered an immense amount of value to said individual or to individuals organization, you will defend yourself to the grain, if you will, of your business on the fact that you will not give this individual a refund on the sole premise that you know you have delivered immense value to this individual. You have spent time, money, resources, effort, your energy, and more importantly, something you can't give back if I didn't already mention what you think I did, which is time once again just to reiterate that you will never give back. And this individual wants to dare rip resource such as the thousands or tens of thousands of dollars that they give to you for the service in which you provided that they are now asking for a refund on from simple unsatisfaction when simply communication needs to be established, delivered to this individual, oh, absolutely. Absolutely not. There's no such thing as a refund in the digital agency space when you are going to the DAP program, ladies and gentlemen. A, because you will make the customer aware before they close that you do not do refunds and that like a marriage, there are no divorces here and you will work out your problems internally or you will go to a counselor in which you will seek marriage counsel in order to make the relationship better because there will be no money given back. If they give you money and they sign an agreement, you verbally make them very aware and you'll have it in your agreement if you use my agreements with specific lingo that will never ever ever enable a refund to be delivered from you ever ever, you will be able to protect yourself from the energy vampires who dare give you resource and then try to take it back. You need to understand that if you protect yourself against these things, obviously you have to be bulletproof on your ability to deliver, I mean, I'm sorry to deliver you. To deliver actual value resources, services and the product in which you sold the individual on, you need to understand that if a person ever requests a refund, you immediately have to know that A, they're unsatisfied, B, there's a specific issue or something that just wasn't delivered for them, it's probably just one little thing. You need to air that out to figure out what it is and you need to get it back from them right away. If anybody ever re-signs with you and then they immediately request a refund, in most cases you've already planned what you're going to do with that money especially after you've transacted it, so once again, it's a problem over you to ever deliver a refund. The only way that you can overcome this issue of somebody ever requested from you, specifically a client or if it's a one-time transactional service, is by protecting yourself legally, by putting in an agreement that there are no refunds because it's an intangible marketing service and number two, that they do not have the ability to slander you in any way, shape or form and that you have that defined in an agreement that they sign. You have to understand once again that if you do not deliver and that you are unethically trying to just hold money for something that you didn't actually do, you are wrong, you are immoral and you do need to give the money back. I don't care if you have an agreement that says no refunds or not, if you have not been able to deliver you have to give the money back. I told a story in one of these previous courses where in months January, February and March of my business, I had times where there were clients that I couldn't deliver for. And ethically, even though I didn't even have the money for another month or two, I had to give the individuals revenue back because I have a policy where I am ethically an integrist inside of my business. However, there are no refunds for anybody who's just unsatisfied all of a sudden because they can't pay their mortgage and need to do a charge back on somebody's card for their largest expense. You will not become that plan B when this person can't pay their bills, fellow DAP member. You have to understand that in business a lot of people unfortunately fall below the 3% line. Literally 97%, 9.7 out of 10 people that you meet are financially scarce and are going to be people who are a little wishy-washy during different times of the month when they have different financial pressures that are closing in on them. You will be one of their highest expenses and you will be somebody that they do try to go back on and take the money away from even though you may be delivering immense value to them. You have to understand that unfortunately there are a lot of that people in the world, like you are a student, you are going to be up against some really interesting and bad people in life unfortunately and you have to be able to legally protect yourself against them. You don't want to wake up with all your accounts negative and slam because somebody did a charge back on you and you couldn't protect yourself against no refund policy. Remember this, this is an intangible marketing service and after services are rendered there can be no refunds due to the fact this is an intangible marketing service with a signed agreement. If you have that exact phrase which I will give you in a document below in a text edit, add it to everything, add it to every invoice you charge, add it to every agreement that you have, just use it in the agreements that I give you and swap out your logos and different client terminology and there and boom! You will be done, covered and protect yourself legally and be able to get your money back every time if anybody charges you back. Also use companies like Stripe, don't ever use Paypal because Paypal is always on the customer side and always make sure that you understand you could get a charge back. Somebody could take the money that gave you away and somebody could completely pull a rug from under your feet when you're not ready for it. Are you going to be prepared? On the refunds, see you in the next video.
